titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 569

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=880 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.361026+00 | translation moa=881 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.378375+00

p.cl: DD dei gratia Rex scottorum . Omnibus Sancte ecclesie fidelibus presentibus et futuris salutem .

Notum sit uobis omnibus quod Robertus
episcopus Sancti Andree inpresentia mea apud Rokesb’ conuocauit Alg’ priorem dunelm’ et Rog’ suppriorem ante hostium
ecclesie Sancti Iohannis euangeliste dicens et contestans se nullam consuetudinem nichil iuris clamare super ecclesiam
de Coldingham . sed uelle et concedere ut ipsa ecclesia libera et quieta esset . ab omni consuetudine et seruitio salua episcopali obedientia . Uolo itaque et firmiter precipio ut ipsa mea elemosina scilicet ecclesia de Coldingham
libera et quieta ab omni consuetudine et seruitio et exactione in perpetuum remaneat monachis Sancti
Cuthberti .

Testibus his . Iohanne episcopo . Roberto de brus . hereberto cancellario Ascelino archidiacono Hugone de moreuilla .
Pagano de braiosa . Hugone brett . Bernegar ingania . Aimaro . et aliis multis .
David by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all Holy Church's faithful, present and to come, greeting.

Let is be known to you all that Robert, bishop of St Andrews, did at Roxburgh in my presence summon Algar, prior of Durham, and Roger, the subprior, before the gate of the church of St John the Evangelist, declaring and bearing witness that he claims no custom and nothing in law against the church of Coldingham, but wills and concedes that the same church be free and quit from every custom and service, except for obedience to the bishop. I therefore will and strictly enjoin that my alms, that is to say, the church of Coldingham, remain to the monks of Saint Cuthbert free and quit in perpetuity from every custom, service, and exaction.
With these witnesses: John the bisho, Robert de Brus, Hertbert the chancellor, Ascelin the archdeacon, Hugh de Morville, Payn de Briouze, Hugh Brett, Berengar Engaine, Aimer, and many others.
